signals.title: Deloitte US eliminates traditional job titles
signals.summary: Effective June 1, 2026, Deloitte's US divisions dropped traditional job titles — explicitly framed as a response to AI absorbing junior consulting work. The most visible job-architecture rebuild in professional services so far.

signals.title: SAP ships suite-wide agentic AI in SuccessFactors
signals.summary: The 1H 2026 release rolls out 15 assistants and 62 agents across the SuccessFactors suite; SmartRecruiters' Winston and SAP Joule begin operating as connected agents. The HRIS layer itself is becoming agentic.

signals.title: ServiceNow's role-scoped HR agents force an HRIS strategy response
signals.summary: ServiceNow's 'autonomous workforce' positioning — agents operating across IT, HR, and security on one platform — pressures Workday, SAP, and Oracle HCM and reopens every HRIS integration roadmap.

signals.title: Workday adds build/connect/monitor tooling for AI agents
signals.summary: June 2026: tools for governing agents that touch employee records, benefits, payroll, and approvals — wired into existing security rules and audit trails. Agent governance is becoming an HRIS feature, not a policy document.

signals.title: Cisco: agent rollout lands two weeks after layoffs begin
signals.summary: Under 4,000 roles cut from July 13; the company-wide AI agent reaches remaining employees about two weeks later. The textbook sequencing collision between restructuring and adoption.

signals.title: EU AI Act: main enforcement wave begins 2 August 2026
signals.summary: Most provisions enforceable from 2 Aug 2026; fines to €35M or 7% of turnover. The Article 4 AI-literacy duty (in force since Feb 2025) makes workforce training a compliance line item across the EU.

signals.title: KPMG Malta: local BCM practice with AI tied to EU transparency law
signals.summary: Member-firm pattern: KPMG Malta runs its own business continuity management practice with AI capabilities aligned to EU transparency requirements — small-market member firms turning regulation into productized method. LEAD — needs source verification.

signals.title: ChatPwC at ~200,000 seats — the largest consulting GenAI deployment
signals.summary: PwC's internal platform reported as OpenAI's largest enterprise customer. The firm's own transformation is the sales credential.

signals.title: McKinsey's Lilli orchestrates ~20,000 agents alongside 40,000 consultants
signals.summary: From 2023 chatbot to multi-model agentic orchestrator powering engagements — an internal agent workforce roughly half the size of the human one.

signals.title: ServiceNow + Accenture launch forward-deployed engineering for agentic AI
signals.summary: Joint program placing forward-deployed engineers inside enterprises to push agents past the pilot phase — the market pricing the pilot-to-production gap as a people-and-deployment problem.

signals.title: Microsoft 2026 Work Trend Index: 'human agency' as the design principle
signals.summary: Microsoft's framing for agent-era work design centers human agency — the vendor-side acknowledgment that adoption is a work-design problem, not a licensing one.

signals.title: Cleveland Clinic: task-based redesign with skills ontologies
signals.summary: Cited in Deloitte's 2026 Human Capital Trends as the emerging practice: couple skills ontologies with task analysis to rebuild roles — not adjust them — around AI.

signals.title: PwC AI Jobs Barometer: AI-exposed roles skew human
signals.summary: New tasks added to AI-exposed roles are 2.5x more likely to rely on empathy, judgement, and creativity; social-emotional skill demand projected +26% (US) and +22% (Europe) by 2030.

signals.title: Gartner: $234B of enterprise app spend at risk from agentic AI
signals.summary: Roughly 20% of enterprise SaaS spend exposed to 'agentic arbitrage' by 2030 — the macro case behind every operating-model conversation this year.
